meta.description: Establish the controlled drying environment after extraction. Perform psychrometric baseline assessment, confirm or adjust engineering controls for the drying phase, define drying goals, and prepare the structure and equipment staging before full drying operations begin.
meta.purpose: Create the conditions for efficient, safe, and documented drying while preventing secondary damage, mold amplification, and cross-contamination. Bridge the gap between water removal and active drying/monitoring.
meta.intended_use: Loaded into field AI voice assistant. Guide technician through psychrometric assessment, control verification, and drying goal setting with clear decision branches and safety gates.

safety_first.hierarchy_of_controls: Maintain engineering controls throughout drying. Engineering > Administrative > PPE. Never relax containment or negative pressure on Category 2/3 or mold jobs during drying.
mandatory_ppe_minimum: Tyvek or equivalent if Category 3, visible mold, or strong odors persist
safety_first.upgrade_triggers: Category 3 water, visible or suspected mold, strong musty/sewage odors, confined space, poor ventilation, or any active items from safety-red-flags.md

flow.title: Pre-Drying Verification & Extraction Sign-Off
flow.voice_prompt: Before setting up drying equipment, confirm that bulk water extraction is complete or sufficiently advanced. Are there any remaining standing water, saturated materials that must come out first, or open red flags?
actions: Walk the affected area and visually confirm no significant standing water remains in accessible locations
Check that all Category 3 or grossly contaminated materials identified for removal have been extracted or isolated
Review any open items from water-extraction.json or initial-assessment.json
Confirm customer has been updated on extraction progress

decision_point.question: Is extraction sufficiently complete to begin controlled drying without creating secondary damage or safety issues?

no_path.action: Return to water-extraction.json or initial-assessment.json to finish critical removal before drying setup.

flow.title: Psychrometric Baseline Assessment
flow.voice_prompt: Take accurate readings of the current environment. Measure temperature, relative humidity, and calculate grains per pound (GPP) or dew point in both affected and unaffected areas. This is your starting point for all drying decisions.
actions: Use a calibrated thermo-hygrometer to record dry-bulb temperature and RH in multiple locations
Calculate or read GPP (grains per pound) and dew point for each zone
Take readings inside containment (if active) and in unaffected reference areas for comparison
Document time, location, and conditions clearly (photo or log)
Note any HVAC status (on/off, what zones it serves)
flow.note: Reference core/psychrometry.md for calculation methods and standard drying targets. Do not rely on 'feels dry' — use numbers.

flow.title: Define Drying Goals & Timeline
flow.voice_prompt: Based on the psychrometric baseline and water category, set realistic drying targets. What moisture content or GPP do we need to reach? How many days is the target drying window?
actions: Determine target equilibrium moisture content (EMC) or standard dry goals for the materials present (drywall, wood, concrete, etc.)
Set target GPP or RH for the drying environment (typically 40-60 GPP or per psychrometry chart for the temperature)
Estimate drying duration using evaporation rate principles from core/psychrometry.md
Factor in water category — Category 3 or mold jobs may require slower, more controlled drying or longer timelines
Document the drying goal and expected completion window for the customer and job file

decision_point.question: Are the drying goals realistic given current conditions, equipment available, and any constraints (power, access, customer timeline)?

no_path.action: Adjust scope, add equipment, or communicate revised timeline to customer before proceeding.

flow.title: Engineering Controls Confirmation & Adjustment for Drying
flow.voice_prompt: Drying phase often requires maintaining or slightly adjusting containment and airflow. Confirm that engineering controls are still appropriate now that we're moving from extraction to evaporation.
actions: Verify containment barriers are still intact and sealed
Confirm negative pressure is holding (target typically -0.02 to -0.05 in. w.c. or per containment-and-controls.json spec)
Check that HEPA air scrubbers are still running and filters are not overloaded
Evaluate if airflow patterns need adjustment for drying (directed drying vs. general ventilation)
Ensure all electrical connections remain GFCI protected in any remaining wet zones
flow.note: On clean Category 1 jobs with no mold risk, you may be able to relax or remove containment earlier. On Category 2/3 or mold jobs, maintain controls throughout drying.

flow.title: Surface Preparation & Initial Airflow Setup
flow.voice_prompt: Prepare surfaces for efficient drying. Remove any remaining thin films of water, open cabinets and drawers, and begin establishing basic airflow patterns before placing full equipment.
actions: Wipe or extract any remaining surface water from floors, baseboards, and accessible surfaces
Open all cabinets, drawers, and closets in affected areas to promote airflow
Remove or elevate contents that block airflow or could trap moisture
Position initial air movers or fans at low velocity to begin gentle air circulation (do not create dust or aerosolize contaminants)
If HVAC is available and safe, evaluate using it for dehumidification or fresh air exchange per psychrometry guidance
red_flag_triggers: Visible mold growth that was not addressed during extraction
Strong odors that intensify when disturbing materials
Electrical hazards when moving equipment or opening walls

flow.title: Equipment Staging & Selection Preparation
flow.voice_prompt: Prepare for equipment placement. Based on the psychrometric assessment and drying goals, identify what type and quantity of equipment will be needed. Do not place equipment yet — that comes in the next procedure.
actions: Estimate required air mover and dehumidifier capacity using psychrometric load calculations
Identify power requirements and confirm GFCI-protected circuits or temporary power plan
Note any access limitations, tripping hazards, or placement constraints
Stage equipment outside containment or in clean staging area until placement is approved
Reference equipment-placement.json for detailed positioning logic and airflow patterns

flow.title: Documentation & Handoff to Drying Operations
flow.voice_prompt: Document everything from this setup phase. Confirm drying goals, baseline conditions, control status, and next steps with the customer and your team.
actions: Photograph baseline conditions and any key findings
Record all psychrometric readings with timestamps and locations
Document drying goals, expected timeline, and equipment plan
Update the job file or digital log with engineering control status
Communicate clearly to customer what to expect during drying (noise, equipment, access needs, timeline)

next_procedures.parallel_or_follow_up: monitoring-and-adjustment.json (start monitoring as soon as equipment is running)

field_notes: Drying setup is the critical bridge between extraction and active drying. The psychrometric baseline (step 2) and drying goals (step 3) are non-negotiable — they prevent both under-drying (secondary damage/mold) and over-drying (unnecessary equipment time and cost). Always maintain engineering controls on contaminated jobs. Never skip the verification in step 4 before moving large volumes of air.
